title: Reaction Type Classifier
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: AP Chemistry
description: Interactive classifier that presents balanced equations and prompts students to identify synthesis, decomposition, single replacement, double replacement, or combustion reactions.

learningObjectives: Identify the five primary reaction classes using structural cues in balanced equations.
Explain the distinguishing feature (reactant count, partner swap, hydrocarbon + O2) that justifies each classification.
prerequisites: Ability to read balanced molecular equations
Knowledge of the five general reaction types

keyQuestions: What feature of the reactants or products tells you the reaction class?
How does the hint align with the formal definition of that reaction type?
commonMisconceptions: All reactions with oxygen are combustion.
Any precipitation reaction must be single replacement.
assessmentOpportunities: Have students record the explanation text after a correct answer as justification.
Ask learners to predict the next reaction type before revealing the equation.
